title: anthurium buganum
intro: Anthurium buganum, also known as the Bugane, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the Arum family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The plant contains medicinal compounds such as alkenes and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reduced fever and relief from coughs. In herbal preparations, Anthurium buganum is often made into a decoction or infusion, which can be consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
